# Winzard delivery contract

## Contract

- Executable work starts from a task brief with a base commit, allowed paths, forbidden paths and required checks.
- Scope expansion, destructive changes and security-boundary changes require explicit approval.
- Implementers produce a structured handoff; a separate review verifies the result.
- Claims of completion are backed by named commands, exit results, commits or artifacts.
- Context access does not grant tool permission or merge authority.

## Acceptance criteria

- A task can be traced to its decisions, specifications, handoff, review and evidence.
